body:
Dear Mr. Holloway,

We were unable to complete your scheduled Bill Pay transfer. Details:

- Payee: Diane Holloway
- Amount: $1,800.00 USD
- Scheduled date: 2027-02-02
- Original transaction reference: txn-corey-holloway-9816fc06
- Return code: R03 (No Account/Unable to Locate Account)
- Reversal posted: 2027-02-04

The funds have been credited back to your Schwab Checking account ending 7231. Please verify the payee routing/account details in your Bill Pay profile and reschedule the payment. If you need help, reply to this thread or call Schwab Bill Pay support at 1-800-435-4000.

Regards,
Schwab Bill Pay Operations
snippet: Failed transfer notice - Alimony $1,800 to Diane Holloway returned; funds credited back to Schwab Checking 7231.
subject: Failed transfer notice - Alimony $1,800 to Diane Holloway returned

body:
Hi Mr. Holloway,

Confirming the reversal from yesterday: $1,800.00 was credited back to your Schwab Checking account ending 7231 on 2027-02-04 as a chargeback of the failed Alimony Bill Pay transfer (original reference txn-corey-holloway-9816fc06).

Your next scheduled Alimony transfer to Diane Holloway remains on file for 2027-03-02. Please update the payee's account details before then to avoid another R03 return.

Thank you,
Schwab Bill Pay Operations

body:
Hello Mr. Holloway,

Your recent lab work at Folsom Family Medical is complete. Please find your results below:

- Hemoglobin A1c: 6.1% (collected 2027-01-13, reported 2027-01-15)
- Fasting glucose: 118 mg/dL
- Fasting lipid panel: TC 192, LDL 108, HDL 54, TG 148

Compared to your prior A1C of 5.8% (Feb 2026), your value has drifted up by 0.3 points into the pre-diabetes range (5.7-6.4%). Dr. Singh will review this with you at your next visit and would like to discuss diet, exercise cadence, and whether a follow-up in 3 months is appropriate.

Please do not reply to this automated message; use the patient portal or call the office to schedule.

Sincerely,
Labs - Folsom Family Medical
snippet: Lab results - A1C 6.1% (Jan 2027), up from 5.8% (Feb 2026); Dr. Singh will review; consider 3-month follow-up.
